codeReview.summary: The prompt and downstream handoff updates look real, but the feature does not fully satisfy the planning-and-handoffs contract. Planning workers are told to use ask_user without actually receiving that tool in the read-only worker allowlist, plan outputs are still only wired into orchestrator artifacts instead of mission artifacts, and the ExitPlanMode handling only exempts ~/.claude/plans sandbox-block text rather than cleanly handling the broader ExitPlanMode/Zod failure mode described by the contract.

issues.description: VAL-PLAN-003 is not met. Read-only planning workers use CLAUDE_READ_ONLY_WORKER_MCP_TOOLS/buildClaudeReadOnlyWorkerAllowedTools, and that allowlist omits mcp__ade__ask_user. The adapter then forces readOnlyExecution workers onto that allowlist at unifiedOrchestratorAdapter.ts:463-465, so planning workers are prompted to use ask_user but cannot actually call it. The test coverage only checks prompt text (planningFlowAndHandoffs.test.ts:267), not runtime tool availability.

VAL-ART-001 is not met. Worker-completion artifact registration still writes only to orchestratorService.registerArtifact (orchestrator_artifacts), while mission artifacts live behind missionService.addArtifact (missionService.ts:3407). No reviewed production path registers a mission artifact of type "plan", and the new test manually inserts an orchestrator artifact instead of verifying mission artifact visibility (planningFlowAndHandoffs.test.ts:465).

VAL-PLAN-002 is only partially handled. classifyBlockingWarnings now skips warnings that contain ~/.claude/plans, but there is still no ExitPlanMode- or Zod-specific handling. completeAttempt() applies this classifier to succeeded attempts (orchestratorService.ts:7288-7290), and the generic blocking patterns in orchestratorQueries.ts:781-789 still treat other ExitPlanMode tool-validation failures as blocking. The added tests cover only the sandbox-path string case (planningFlowAndHandoffs.test.ts:224-263), not the Zod error path required by the contract.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The current service-worker guidance was not enough to keep the worker's tests aligned with the runtime contract surfaces for this feature: it accepted prompt-text coverage for tool availability and registry-level coverage for mission artifact visibility.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: planningFlowAndHandoffs.test.ts:267 only asserts that the prompt mentions ask_user, while unifiedOrchestratorAdapter.ts:155-163 and 463-465 show the read-only worker allowlist still omits it. planningFlowAndHandoffs.test.ts:465 manually calls orchestratorService.registerArtifact(... kind: 'custom'), while missionService.ts:3407 is the actual mission-artifact API used for Evidence/Artifacts.

summary: Fail. The feature improves planning prompts and handoff summary plumbing, but it still misses the actual planner ask_user tool wiring, does not register plan outputs as mission artifacts, and does not demonstrate clean handling of the broader ExitPlanMode/Zod error path.
